authoritative_sources.title: Clothes Captioning: Complying with the Care Labeling Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. public authority for apparel care-label rules. The guide defines label permanence, reasonable basis for care instructions, warnings, and when care-label evidence is mandatory.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fit-evidence validation and care-label dispute handling
used_for: when missing care instructions block a confident answer

authoritative_sources.title: Threading Your Way Through the Labeling Requirements Under the Textile and Wool Acts
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the controlling public authority for textile and wool labeling obligations. The guide defines fiber-content, country-of-origin, and responsible-party labeling requirements used to ground apparel and textile return evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Item-identity validation and label-sensitive exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: Business Guide to the FTC's Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal consumer-protection authority for remote merchandise orders. This guide defines shipment substantiation, supply expectations, delay notices, and prompt refund behavior for exchange and replacement workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Availability assessment and remote-order exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: California Civil Code Section 1723
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The California Legislature publishes the controlling statutory text for refund-policy posting and fallback cash, credit, or exchange outcomes, making it a direct jurisdictional control for apparel and footwear returns.

authoritative_sources.title: Clothing Business Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the public safety authority for apparel flammability rules. The guidance distinguishes general apparel, exempt footwear, and children's sleepwear sizing and testing posture, which directly affects safe routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Safety-boundary review for apparel and sleepwear returns

authoritative_sources.title: Clothing Color & Size Codes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US is the recognized U.S. retail standards body for trading-partner data exchange. Its color and size code system creates the common language used across apparel, textile, and footwear product master data.
used_for: footwear and apparel size category awareness

authoritative_sources.title: Guideline for Floor Ready Merchandise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US publishes this vendor-neutral retail workflow guidance for ticketing, tagging, shipping, receiving, and store presentation across apparel and footwear trading partners.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Vendor, pre-ticketing, and shipment exception handling
used_for: vendor mis-pack and receiving blocker handling

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practice Guideline for Exchanging Product Images and Attributes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 US publishes the product-image and attribute exchange workflow that suppliers and retailers use for commerce applications, listing, and fit-relevant product information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fit-detail evidence, image handling, and product-attribute validation
used_for: product image and attribute artifact expectations
style, GTIN, and attribute linkage across trading partners

authoritative_sources.title: ASTM D5585-21 Standard Tables of Body Measurements for Adult Female Misses Figure Type, Size Range 00-20
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTM is a recognized standards body. D5585-21 states that standardized body measurements assist manufacturers in reducing consumer confusion and dissatisfaction related to apparel sizing.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 19408:2023 Footwear — Sizing — Vocabulary
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO is the recognized international standards body. ISO 19408:2023 defines the vocabulary for measuring feet and lasts and for determining footwear size.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 19407:2023 Footwear — Sizing — Conversion of sizing systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 19407:2023 is the recognized published standard for footwear size conversion across major sizing systems, including the United States, making it directly relevant to conversion-based exchanges.
used_for: when footwear conversion uncertainty should be recorded instead of guessed

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and specialty grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm channel, product class, style-color-size-width context, return reason, jurisdiction, and tenant authority map before deciding whether the request is in scope or blocked.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Fit and evidence prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Verify size charts, fit notes, care-label and fiber-label artifacts, product images, condition evidence, and order linkage so the lane does not generalize from garment labels or shopper size language alone.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Availability and exchange or replenishment assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare style-color-size-width inventory, reservations, vendor commitments, shipment status, and remote-order timing rules to determine whether a replacement or replenishment action is supportable.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Vendor, ticketing, and shipment action management
workflow_stages.description: Package bounded vendor, pre-ticketing, floor-ready, and receiving actions when mis-packs, wrong size runs, or shipment delays block execution.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception escalation for policy, safety, and authority shifts
workflow_stages.description: Stop or escalate when shortages, care-label disputes, missing traceability, sleepwear or safety questions, hygiene exclusions, or substitution pressure make the answer depend on an adjacent owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: style-color-size-width product-master records with SKU, GTIN, UPC, vendor style, and assortment hierarchy
size charts, fit notes, size-conversion tables, product images, and consumer-measurement or fit-assessment records where the tenant captures them
care labels, fiber-content labels, country-of-origin records, RN or responsible-party identifiers, and condition or laundering evidence
POS receipts, OMS orders, RMAs, BOPIS or ship-from-store records, payment status, and return-reason notes
inventory, WMS, ERP, and transfer records by style-color-size-width with on-hand, reserved, in-transit, and unavailable states
purchase orders, ASNs, carton-pack data, floor-ready or pre-ticketing specifications, vendor acknowledgements, and lead-time updates
tenant return-policy matrices, hygiene exclusions, exception-approval records, and specialized-footwear or safety handling notes
availability summaries, procurement recommendations, vendor action notes, and shortage or exception escalations produced by the lane
systems_of_record: vendor portal, EDI, or ASN feed
compliance or safety incident system when applicable

allowed: Classify apparel, textile, and footwear requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Prepare evidence-backed availability summaries, vendor notes, procurement recommendations, and shortage escalations without overstating authority.
Determine whether documented inventory, vendor, or replenishment conditions support a standard exchange or follow-up action.
escalate: Required style-color-size, care-label, fit, inventory, or vendor records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires pricing override, spend approval, policy exception approval, legal interpretation, safety review, or licensed fit authority.
A public baseline and tenant, marketplace, franchise, or jurisdiction-specific policy materially conflict.
Children's sleepwear, protective footwear, hygiene restrictions, or suspected mislabeling change whether routine handling is safe.
refuse: Force an exchange or replenishment recommendation without authoritative size, fit, or availability evidence.
Approve substitutions, refunds, or exceptions outside documented authority.
Treat safety-sensitive, compliance-sensitive, or specialized-fit products as ordinary apparel returns.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a replacement size or width even though authoritative style-color-size inventory or conversion records do not support it
a care-label or shrinkage dispute is handled without care instructions, laundering evidence, or fiber-content records
vendor mis-pack, pre-ticketing, or carton-pack problems are treated as ordinary returns and the wrong size run is restocked or reordered
children's sleepwear or protective footwear questions are handled as generic fit complaints instead of safety-boundary escalations
the lane overgeneralizes brand-specific or style-specific fit and tells the requester to size up or down without the underlying artifacts
intimate-apparel, swimwear, worn, washed, or altered-item restrictions are bypassed without the required tenant policy and approval evidence
apparel, textile, and footwear returns drift into generic customer-service language instead of using style-color-size, care-label, ticketing, and fit evidence

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Brand-specific fit models, vanity sizing, width conventions, stretch behavior, and alteration rules vary materially by tenant, brand, and product class.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public ASTM, ISO, and GS1 sources provide vocabulary and baseline structure, but runtime must retrieve the tenant's actual size charts, fit heuristics, and product-class rules before finalizing fit-sensitive decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Refund-policy and hygiene restrictions vary by jurisdiction, channel, marketplace program, franchise model, and item class.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: California Civil Code Section 1723 provides a public jurisdictional example, but runtime must retrieve the applicable tenant and jurisdiction policy before concluding on return eligibility.

notes: The source set covers policy, workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than relying on a single regulator or a generic retail source.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated package because the unresolved items are explicitly delegated to tenant retrieval rather than guessed.
